title: Top 10 Herbal Creams for Frozen Shoulder - The Ultimate List

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and stiffness in the affected shoulder joint.

Polyphenols, the main compound found in green tea, help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals and improving blood flow to the affected area.
Tannins are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area, thus improving flexibility and range of motion.
Demethoxycurcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the shoulder joint, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream is a natural remedy that may help with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and hard to move. The active ingredient in Curcuma longa, called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. When applied topically as a cream, curcumin can penetrate deep into the skin and reach the inflamed tissues in the shoulder joint. It can also help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to have a positive effect on the production of collagen, a protein that helps to repair damaged tissue. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa cream may help to improve flexibility and mobility in the shoulder joint, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. Regular application of the cream may also help to reduce the risk of further inflammation and promote long-term healing in the affected area.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that can help with frozen shoulder, you'll need to start by creating a tea infusion from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, also known as green tea. This involves boiling one cup of water and then adding one teaspoon of dried Camellia sinensis leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the liquid and letting it cool. Next, you'll need to mix the cooled tea infusion with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:1 ratio, creating a base for your cream. You can then add other natural ingredients like beeswax, shea butter, and essential oils to enhance the cream's texture and benefits. For frozen shoulder, you may want to add anti-inflammatory ingredients such as arnica or ginger. Once you've mixed all the ingredients together, heat them gently until the beeswax has melted, then pour the mixture into a container and let it cool before use. This Camellia sinensis cream can be applied directly to the affected area to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area, making it easier to move and recover from the condition.
Shogaol is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected joint, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.
Zingiberene is good for frozen shoulder because it reduces inflammation and pain by blocking the production of chemicals that cause swelling and discomfort in the affected area.
Gingerols may be good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the shoulder area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, can be beneficial for people with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move the arm. The cream's active ingredient, ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. When applied topically, the cream can help relax the muscles and improve blood circulation, which in turn can help ease stiffness and discomfort. The cream's warming sensation can also help increase flexibility and range of motion in the shoulder joint. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that can help protect the joint from further damage and promote healing. Regular use of Zingiber officinale cream may help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms and improve overall mobility and comfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, start by infusing the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. This process is called cold infusing and it's done by placing the leaves in a clean glass jar, covering them with the carrier oil, and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, add a few drops of essential oils like peppermint or eucalyptus to help reduce pain and inflammation, and mix well. Finally, combine the infused oil with a natural thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter, and heat the mixture gently until the beeswax or shea butter is fully melted. Pour the mixture into small glass jars, let it cool, and your Camellia sinensis herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help reduce pain and inflammation, and promote healing and flexibility in the shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the shoulder joint, allowing for easier movement and improving overall flexibility.
Polyphenols may help alleviate frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected joint, which can promote healing and reduce pain.
Tannins are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana cream is a popular remedy for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The cream contains compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to decrease inflammation and promote healing in the body. When applied topically to the affected shoulder, the cream can help reduce stiffness and pain, making it easier to move the shoulder joint. Arnica montana cream also contains antioxidants that help protect the tissue from further damage, promoting faster recovery. Many people have found relief from frozen shoulder symptoms after using Arnica montana cream regularly, and it's often recommended by healthcare professionals as a natural alternative to medication. The cream can be applied 2-3 times a day, and it's usually gentle enough for daily use without any side effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, you will need dried Camellia sinensis leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a few basic kitchen tools like a double boiler, a cheesecloth, and a glass jar for storage. First, steep the dried Camellia sinensis leaves in the carrier oil in the double boiler for 2-3 hours or overnight, allowing the oil to absorb the herbal properties. Next, strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the leaves. Discard the solids and let the oil cool. Once cooled, add a few drops of a pain-relieving essential oil like peppermint or eucalyptus to enhance the cream's benefits. Finally, whip the oil with a hand mixer or blender until it becomes light and fluffy, then transfer it to the glass jar for storage. To use, massage a small amount of the cream onto the affected shoulder area 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Capsaicin is not found. Camellia sinensis is the plant from which tea is made, while Capsaicin is actually found in chili peppers. However, Capsaicin in chili peppers has been found to be good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Quercetin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling in the affected shoulder joint.
Polyphenols have been found to be good for frozen shoulder because they can help reduce inflammation and pain, thereby improving mobility and flexibility in the affected joint.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum cream is made from the Capsicum annuum plant, which contains a chemical called capsaicin. This chemical has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce swelling and pain in the body. When used on a frozen shoulder, the capsaicin in the cream can help to reduce pain and stiffness by blocking the pain signals sent to the brain. This can help to relax the muscles and improve movement in the shoulder. The cream can also help to break down adhesions and scar tissue that can cause stiffness and pain in a frozen shoulder. Additionally, the heat from the capsaicin can increase blood flow to the area, which can help to promote healing and reduce pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, start by preparing the herbal base. Begin by infusing dried Camellia sinensis leaves, also known as green tea leaves, in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Combine one cup of dried Camellia sinensis leaves with two cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil extract the herbal properties. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, combine the infused oil with a moisturizing cream base, such as shea butter or cocoa butter, and a few drops of a natural emulsifier like beeswax. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is well combined. Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool before use. Once cooled, the Camellia sinensis herbal cream is ready to be applied topically to the affected area to help reduce inflammation and promote healing for frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Capsicum annuum herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is often used to help people with frozen shoulder. This is because the cream contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation in the affected area. When you have frozen shoulder, the joint becomes stiff and painful due to inflammation. The glycyrrhizin in the cream helps calm down the inflammation, making it easier to move the joint. It also helps relax the muscles around the joint, which can become tight and painful. By reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, the cream can help improve range of motion and reduce pain in the shoulder. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has anti-oxidant properties that help protect the joint from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, the cream can help promote healing and recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams for frozen shoulder relief, start by infusing the Camellia sinensis leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1 part of dried Camellia sinensis leaves with 2 parts of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it sit in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the leaves infuse their properties into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax and shea butter to create a creamy texture. Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring until the beeswax and shea butter melt, then let it cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container. This Camellia sinensis herbal cream can be applied topically to the affected area to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream can be helpful in reducing pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder. The main ingredient in this cream, eucalyptus oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the affected area. When applied topically, it can reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the shoulder joint. The cooling sensation of eucalyptus oil can also help numb the area, providing temporary relief from pain. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus cream may increase blood flow to the area, which can aid in the healing process and reduce scar tissue formation. By reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting healing, eucalyptus globulus cream may help improve range of motion and reduce stiffness in frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, you'll need to start by infusing the tea leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or sweet almond oil. Fill a small saucepan with the oil and add 1-2 teaspoons of Camellia sinensis tea leaves for every 8 ounces of oil. Heat the oil over low heat, then let it simmer for about 2-3 hours, or until the oil has taken on a rich, green color and a strong tea scent. Once the oil has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the tea leaves. Next, combine the infused oil with a carrier like beeswax or shea butter to create a thick, moisturizing cream. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture has thickened. Finally, transfer the cream to a clean jar and store it in the fridge, where it will thicken further and become ready to use. To apply the cream to a frozen shoulder, simply massage it into the affected area, where it will help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium cream, also known as yarrow, is a natural remedy that can help with frozen shoulder. This condition occurs when the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it difficult to move the arm. The cream contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When applied to the affected area, it can reduce swelling and ease pain, allowing for better mobility and relaxation of the muscles. Yarrow cream also has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles and tendons surrounding the shoulder joint, making it easier to move the arm. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce the formation of scar tissue that can contribute to frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, start by brewing a strong tea from dried Camellia sinensis leaves. This herbal tea is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that will help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder. Combine 1 tablespoon of dried Camellia sinensis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a bowl. Next, mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil or shea butter with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Add 2 tablespoons of the brewed Camellia sinensis tea to the mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully melted. Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 130°F. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or tin container and let it cool and solidify completely. Once set, your Camellia sinensis herbal cream is ready to use. Gently massage a small amount onto the affected shoulder area to help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Frozen shoulder is a painful condition where the shoulder joint gets inflamed and stiff. Aloe barbadensis cream can help with this condition in several ways. The cream contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. This can help to ease the stiffness and discomfort caused by frozen shoulder. The cream also has moisturizing properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin, which can become dry and irritated due to the condition. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in the cream can help to reduce scar tissue that can form in the shoulder joint, making it easier to move the arm and shoulder. This can also help to reduce the risk of long-term damage to the joint and surrounding tissues. Using Aloe barbadensis cream regularly may help to reduce the symptoms of frozen shoulder and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal cream for frozen shoulder, you'll need a few basic ingredients and some simple equipment. First, start by making a strong tea infusion from 2 teaspoons of dried Camellia sinensis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid and set it aside to cool. Next, mix 1/4 cup of cooled tea with 1/2 cup of a base cream, such as coconut or shea butter, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until the cream is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of around 120°F. Remove from heat and let it cool to around 100°F before pouring it into a clean glass jar. Add a few drops of an essential oil like eucalyptus or peppermint, if desired, for their anti-inflammatory properties. Let the cream cool and solidify completely before applying it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

remedies.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens cream is sometimes used to help people with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful. The cream is made from the leaves of the Gaultheria procumbens plant, which is also known as wintergreen. The plant contains a chemical called methyl salicylate, which is similar to aspirin. Methyl salicylate can help reduce pain and inflammation in the body. When applied to the skin, the cream may help reduce pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint, making it easier to move. Some people also believe that the cream may help relax muscles and improve circulation, which can help reduce pain and stiffness. However, more research is needed to fully understand the benefits of Gaultheria procumbens cream for frozen shoulder. The cream is usually applied directly to the affected area, and some people may experience a warm or tingling sensation after application. Gaultheria procumbens cream can be found in some health food stores or online, but it's essential to talk to a doctor before using it, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Camellia sinensis leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil or grape seed extract. First, steep the dried Camellia sinensis leaves in the carrier oil for a few weeks, shaking the mixture daily, to create a strong tea. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the leaves, then discard the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler and slowly add the Camellia sinensis oil, stirring constantly. Add the preservative and continue stirring until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Camellia sinensis herbal cream is ready to use. To apply, massage a small amount onto the affected shoulder, focusing on the areas of pain and stiffness. Repeat as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day, for best results. Note: Before using any new skincare product, especially one made from herbs, do a patch test to ensure you don't have any sensitivity or allergic reactions.
remedies.image_alt: Gaultheria procumbens herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you need to make a Camellia sinensis infusion by steeping one teaspoon of Camellia sinensis leaves in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. intro_desc: Frozen shoulder is a condition where the joint capsule around the shoulder becomes inflamed and tight, causing pain and stiffness. This can make everyday activities like dressing, grooming, and even sleeping uncomfortable. To alleviate this condition, herbal creams can be an effective solution. One such cream combines Curcuma longa and Zingiber officinale, two herbs with anti-inflammatory properties. Curcuma longa, commonly known as turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin that reduces inflammation and pain. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has a similar effect, and its warming properties can help relax the muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area.  Applying a cream containing these herbs to the affected area can help reduce inflammation and pain, making it easier to move the shoulder and perform daily activities. This can improve your quality of life by reducing discomfort and allowing you to engage in hobbies and exercise without pain. If left untreated, frozen shoulder can lead to chronic pain, limited mobility, and decreased range of motion. By using herbal creams, you can address the root cause of the condition and start feeling better sooner. Regular application can help break the cycle of inflammation and pain, allowing you to regain control over your daily activities and improve your overall well-being.
supplementary_best: A combination of Arnica montana, Camellia sinensis, and Gaultheria procumbens herbal creams may help remedy frozen shoulder. Arnica montana cream reduces inflammation and pain, which are common symptoms of frozen shoulder. Camellia sinensis cream, also known as green tea cream, contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area. Gaultheria procumbens cream, or wintergreen cream, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help relax muscles and reduce pain.  When used together, these creams may work synergistically to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, making it easier to move the shoulder and regain mobility. Arnica montana cream can help reduce inflammation, while Camellia sinensis cream can help promote healing, and Gaultheria procumbens cream can help relax muscles.  This combination can be applied topically to the affected area 2-3 times a day, ideally after physical therapy or warm baths to increase blood flow and reduce stiffness.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a frozen shoulder, there are certain herbal creams you should avoid. Some of these creams contain peppermint oil, which can numb the area and make it harder to move. This can worsen the condition. Another herb to avoid is tea tree oil, which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions, especially if you have sensitive skin. Chamomile oil, while generally soothing, can also cause skin irritation in some people. Valerian root can be too sedating and may make you feel dizzy or lightheaded, which can be a problem if you have a frozen shoulder and need to keep moving your arm. Comfrey oil has been linked to liver damage if used in large amounts or for extended periods, which can be a concern if you're using it to treat a long-term condition like frozen shoulder. Finally, calendula oil can be irritating to skin that's already inflamed or sensitive.
supplementary_other_remedies: Applying heat or cold can help reduce pain and stiffness in a frozen shoulder. Heat therapy, like a warm bath or shower, can loosen up tight muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area. On the other hand, cold therapy, such as an ice pack or cold compress, can help reduce inflammation and numb the pain. Some people also find relief by using contrast water therapy, which involves alternating between hot and cold water to promote circulation and healing.  Another natural remedy is the use of Epsom salt in warm water baths. The magnesium in Epsom salt can help relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation, all of which can help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms. Some people also find it helpful to use a foam roller or massage ball to gently roll out and stretch their shoulder muscles. This can help break up scar tissue and adhesions that can contribute to frozen shoulder pain.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with frozen shoulder often experience other ailments due to inflammation and restricted movement in the affected joint. One common condition is bursitis, where the fluid-filled sacs (bursae) surrounding the joint become inflamed, causing pain and swelling. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties, while ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds called gingerols and shogaols.  Another condition people with frozen shoulder may experience is tendinitis, where the tendons surrounding the joint become inflamed, leading to pain and stiffness. Medicinal herbs like willow bark and meadowsweet can help alleviate pain and inflammation. Willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation. Meadowsweet contains salicylic acid, another anti-inflammatory compound that can help relieve pain and stiffness.  Some people with frozen shoulder may also experience osteoarthritis, a condition where the cartilage in the joint wears down, causing pain and stiffness. Medicinal herbs like boswellia and ashwagandha can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Boswellia contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties, while ashwagandha has anti-inflammatory compounds called withanolides.  These medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. They work by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation, and by promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
